question: Will Elon Musk post 580+ tweets from March 20 to March 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 20 12:00 PM ET to March 27, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters around 320-359 tweets for Elon Musk's March 20-27, 2026 week, implying a daily pace of 40-45 posts, mirroring his 2024 average of 35-50 amid political firestorms and X platform tweaks. Recent surges—like 98 posts in a day during election hype—bolster mid-range bets, while quieter periods (under 300 weekly) during Tesla launches temper extremes. With no confirmed 2026 events like Mars updates or midterms disrupting his rhythm, leading ranges compete tightly on baseline habits versus viral catalysts, as cultural icon status fuels unpredictable spikes in public discourse. Volatility persists, with traders eyeing historical precedents for over/under thresholds.

question: Spread: AZ (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 10:45 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "AZ" if AZ win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "SC Heerenveen".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AZ vs. SC Heerenveen - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 10:45 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: AZ Alkmaar's strong home form and third-place standing in the Eredivisie drive trader consensus favoring them against mid-table SC Heerenveen, with markets reflecting about 65% implied probability for an AZ win. Alkmaar enters unbeaten in their last five league matches, scoring freely thanks to Troy Parrott's eight goals, while Heerenveen has lost four of six away games, conceding 2.2 goals per match on average. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Heerenveen's suspension of key midfielder Simon Olsson could disrupt their midfield control. Upcoming factors include AZ's rest advantage after midweek cup action and Heerenveen's poor head-to-head record (winless in last five visits to AFAS Stadion), tilting sentiment toward the hosts despite Eredivisie volatility.

events.title: Deportivo Toluca FC vs. Atlético San Luis - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga MX game, scheduled for April 12 at 9:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Toluca's dominant home form in Liga MX Apertura, unbeaten in six matches at Estadio Nemesio Díez, anchors trader consensus favoring them over visiting Atlético San Luis, who have just one away win this season. Toluca hold second place after three consecutive victories, scoring freely with 14 goals in their last five games, while San Luis sit 12th amid three straight losses and defensive woes conceding 2.2 goals per match lately. Official injury reports confirm no new absences for Toluca's key attackers like Alexis Vega, but San Luis misses midfielder Vitinho. Head-to-head history tilts Toluca's way (4-1 in last five), with rest advantage post-midweek for the hosts potentially swaying probabilities.

events.title: ACF Fiorentina vs. FC Internazionale Milano - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Serie A game between ACF Fiorentina and FC Internazionale Milano, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 3:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Milan's attacking duo of Lautaro Martinez and Marcus Thuram drives much of the player prop sentiment, with Martinez's league-leading 10 goals in 7 matches making his anytime scorer market a trader focal point amid Inter's 5-game win streak. Fiorentina counters with Moise Kean in form (4 goals recently), but face absences like defender Dodô (hamstring) and midfielder Mandragora (doubtful per official reports), potentially inflating Inter shots-on-target props for Barella and Dimarco. Recent head-to-heads favor Inter 3-1 aggregate, with home edge at Artemio Franchi tilting toward Viola corners and cards; rest advantage to visitors after midweek rest, though upsets loom in this rivalry clash.

question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 17°C or below on March 21?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 21 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' overwhelming 92% consensus on 22°C as Hong Kong's highest temperature on March 21 stems from the Hong Kong Observatory's (HKO) latest forecast, projecting sunny intervals with a daytime maximum of exactly 22 degrees Celsius under stable high-pressure influence. This aligns with recent mild weather patterns, where March historical maxima average 21-23°C, supported by consistent numerical weather prediction models showing minimal cloud cover and light winds favoring steady warming. Scenarios challenging this include an unexpected cold front arrival—lowering peaks below 20°C—or rare hot northerly winds pushing toward 23°C, though HKO ensemble forecasts indicate under 5% probability for deviations beyond 1°C.

question: Spread: AC Goianiense (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 12 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AC Goianiense" if AC Goianiense win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Londrina EC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: AC Goianiense vs. Londrina EC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 12 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Goianiense holds a clear edge as the home side in this Copa do Brasil third-round clash, with trader consensus pricing them at around 65% implied probability to advance following their solid 2-0 second-leg win over Londrina last week, securing a 4-2 aggregate victory. Recent developments include ACG's Shaylon returning from suspension to bolster midfield creativity, while Londrina battles key absences in defender Gabrielzinho (hamstring) and forward Caprini (doubtful per official reports), exposing defensive frailties evident in their leaky backline during Série B struggles. Londrina's momentum waned post-relegation fight, contrasting ACG's Serie A rest advantage and unbeaten home streak; watch for weather in Goiânia potentially favoring the hosts' pressing style.

The hosts sit 11th with an unbeaten streak in five home matches, fresh off a 1-0 upset over Millonarios, while Alianza languishes near relegation in 17th, dropping seven straight road games due to porous defense conceding 2.3 goals per away outing. No significant injuries hit La Equidad per official reports, but Alianza suspends top scorer Kevin Viveros. Head-to-head trends show La Equidad winning the last three encounters, with both squads eyeing crucial points amid tightening Clausura standings.

events.title: CR Brasil vs. Athletic Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 12 at 7:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: CR Brasil enters this Serie C showdown with momentum from a three-match unbeaten streak, including a 2-0 home win over a direct rival, bolstering trader consensus on their 55% implied win probability amid home advantage at Estádio José Cavalcante. Athletic Club's defensive frailties—conceding 1.8 goals per away game—worsen with confirmed suspension of starting center-back Léo Costa per official report, while CR Brasil regains forward Lohan from injury. Recent form shows CR Brasil's superior head-to-head edge (2 wins in last 3 meetings), but upcoming schedule congestion for both could spark an upset, as Athletic Club's counterattacking threat persists despite road woes. Market odds reflect crowd wisdom on these roster shifts.

In the upcoming Brazil Serie B game between CR Brasil and Athletic Club, scheduled for April 12 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if CR Brasil and Athletic Club combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CR Brasil vs. Athletic Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 12 at 7:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: CR Brasil enters this Serie C showdown with momentum from a three-match unbeaten streak, including a 2-0 home win over a direct rival, bolstering trader consensus on their 55% implied win probability amid home advantage at Estádio José Cavalcante. Athletic Club's defensive frailties—conceding 1.8 goals per away game—worsen with confirmed suspension of starting center-back Léo Costa per official report, while CR Brasil regains forward Lohan from injury. Recent form shows CR Brasil's superior head-to-head edge (2 wins in last 3 meetings), but upcoming schedule congestion for both could spark an upset, as Athletic Club's counterattacking threat persists despite road woes. Market odds reflect crowd wisdom on these roster shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers hold a commanding 3-2 series lead over the New York Rangers in the NHL Eastern Conference Finals, with Game 6 tonight at Madison Square Garden giving the Rangers a must-win home opportunity to force a decisive seventh game. eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their home clash with the Philadelphia Flyers, driven by superior roster depth and defensive prowess allowing under two goals per game through early season play. Key recent development: forward Andrei Svechnikov's full return from offseason surgery strengthens Carolina's top-six forwards, while Flyers' goaltender Samuel Ersson remains day-to-day with a lower-body issue, forcing reliance on unproven backup Ivan Fedotov. Philly's road woes persist with a 0-2-0 away record and middling power play, contrasting the Canes' 5-1-0 start and rest advantage. Schedule density for Philadelphia ahead could amplify upset risk, but historical dominance favors Carolina 7-3 in last 10 meetings.

Recent developments include Dallas' deep playoff run to the Western Conference Final against Edmonton, showcasing road resilience, while Toronto licks wounds from a first-round loss to Boston amid goaltending instability. Head-to-head regular season splits favored Dallas in tight OT wins; watch Leafs' offensive stars Matthews and Nylander for counterpunch potential in this potential rivalry revival.

eventMetadata.context_description: Minnesota Wild hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their Central Division matchup against the St. Louis Blues, with implied probabilities reflecting the Wild's hot streak of four wins in five games versus the Blues' three losses in four. Recent developments favor Minnesota: full health on injury reports, including Kirill Kaprizov suiting up after preseason tweaks, while St. Louis scratches forward Zack Bolduc with illness. Filip Gustavsson's .925 save percentage anchors the Wild's stout defense, contrasting Jordan Binnington's recent .890 clip. Blues' home-ice edge at Enterprise Center tempers the line, but Wild's 3-1 record in last four head-to-heads underscores momentum heading into this early-season test.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Blackhawks hold a slim edge in trader consensus for their home matchup against the Buffalo Sabres, driven by Connor Bedard's offensive spark amid Chicago's desperate push to snap a three-game skid (1-4-1 record). Buffalo counters with superior depth and recent momentum from a 3-1-1 stretch, but faces uncertainty with Tage Thompson day-to-day (upper-body injury per official report) and Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's hot streak in net (2.21 GAA). Blackhawks' Petr Mrazek has faltered lately (4.50 GAA), exposing defensive woes, while Sabres enjoy a rest advantage. Head-to-head history favors Chicago at home, but Buffalo's penalty kill (84.6%) could neutralize power plays—monitor final lineups for shifts in implied probabilities.

question: Will Israel launch a ground operation in Iran by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if participation by Israeli military personnel in a ground operation in Iran on or after February 28, 2026, is confirmed by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A "ground operation" is defined as a military operation that involves Israeli military ground forces conducting operations within Iranian territory.

For the purposes of this market, aerial and maritime operations will not be considered. Only operations involving Israeli troops on the ground within the terrestrial territory of Iran will qualify.

Official confirmation by the Israeli military of personnel operating on the ground within Iranian territory during the specified timeframe will resolve this market immediately. An overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming that the Israeli military personnel operated on the ground within Iranian territory during the specified timeframe will also suffice.

Qualifying confirmations include statements such as the June 25, 2025, statement by IDF Chief of Staff Lt.-Gen. Eyal Zamir confirmed that Israeli commando forces operated covertly on the ground inside Iran during the 12-day war.

Confirmations referring only to non-military Israeli intelligence activity, including operations by the Mossad or Shin Bet, as well as airstrikes, cyberattacks, standoff weapons, remote sabotage, or actions by proxies or third parties without Israeli military presence, will not alone qualify.

Incidents that would not alone suffice include the November 2025 Mossad statement indicating its agents were present during protests in Iran, or the July 31, 2024, assassination of Hamas leader Ismail Haniyeh in Tehran, widely attributed to Mossad agents.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information by the Israeli military; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus shows near-zero implied probability for confirmation of an Israeli ground operation inside Iran, driven by the complete absence of official statements from Israeli Defense Forces, US officials, or Iranian authorities amid unverified rumors. Recent developments include Israel's limited airstrikes on Iranian military sites on October 26, 2024, following Tehran's missile barrage earlier that month, but all actions have remained aerial or proxy-based via Lebanon and Syria. Escalation risks persist with Israel's ground push against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, yet a direct incursion into Iran would mark unprecedented war. Traders eye upcoming US election outcomes and potential IAEA reports on Iran's nuclear program as key catalysts that could shift odds.

Qualifying confirmations include statements such as the June 25, 2025, statement by IDF Chief of Staff Lt.-Gen. Eyal Zamir confirmed that Israeli commando forces operated covertly on the ground inside Iran during the 12-day war.

Confirmations referring only to non-military Israeli intelligence activity, including operations by the Mossad or Shin Bet, as well as airstrikes, cyberattacks, standoff weapons, remote sabotage, or actions by proxies or third parties without Israeli military presence, will not alone qualify.

Incidents that would not alone suffice include the November 2025 Mossad statement indicating its agents were present during protests in Iran, or the July 31, 2024, assassination of Hamas leader Ismail Haniyeh in Tehran, widely attributed to Mossad agents.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information by the Israeli military; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

events.title: Israel ground operation in Iran confirmed by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if participation by Israeli military personnel in a ground operation in Iran on or after February 28, 2026, is confirmed by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A "ground operation" is defined as a military operation that involves Israeli military ground forces conducting operations within Iranian territory.

For the purposes of this market, aerial and maritime operations will not be considered. Only operations involving Israeli troops on the ground within the terrestrial territory of Iran will qualify.

Official confirmation by the Israeli military of personnel operating on the ground within Iranian territory during the specified timeframe will resolve this market immediately. The primary resolution source for this market will be official information by the Israeli military; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus shows near-zero implied probability for confirmation of an Israeli ground operation inside Iran, driven by the complete absence of official statements from Israeli Defense Forces, US officials, or Iranian authorities amid unverified rumors. Recent developments include Israel's limited airstrikes on Iranian military sites on October 26, 2024, following Tehran's missile barrage earlier that month, but all actions have remained aerial or proxy-based via Lebanon and Syria. Escalation risks persist with Israel's ground push against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, yet a direct incursion into Iran would mark unprecedented war. Traders eye upcoming US election outcomes and potential IAEA reports on Iran's nuclear program as key catalysts that could shift odds.

question: Will Iran conduct a military action against Israel on March 19, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Israel’s soil on the listed date in Israel Time (GMT+2). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact Israeli ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of Israel counts. Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Israeli territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Iranian ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after the specified date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for Iranian military action against Israel in the near term, driven primarily by Tehran's restrained response to Israel's October 2024 airstrikes on Iranian missile sites, which avoided nuclear or oil facilities. Iran's Supreme Leader has vowed revenge but emphasized proportionality amid economic pressures and proxy escalations via Hezbollah and Houthis. Recent IAEA reports highlight Iran's uranium enrichment advances, heightening diplomatic tensions, while U.S. election outcomes could influence deterrence signals. Upcoming events include potential UN Security Council sessions and Israeli operations in Gaza/Lebanon, which may either de-escalate or provoke further rhetoric without direct strikes.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after the specified date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for Iranian military action against Israel in the near term, driven primarily by Tehran's restrained response to Israel's October 2024 airstrikes on Iranian missile sites, which avoided nuclear or oil facilities. Iran's Supreme Leader has vowed revenge but emphasized proportionality amid economic pressures and proxy escalations via Hezbollah and Houthis. Recent IAEA reports highlight Iran's uranium enrichment advances, heightening diplomatic tensions, while U.S. election outcomes could influence deterrence signals. Upcoming events include potential UN Security Council sessions and Israeli operations in Gaza/Lebanon, which may either de-escalate or provoke further rhetoric without direct strikes.

question: Will Iran conduct a military action against Israel on March 29, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Israel’s soil on the listed date in Israel Time (GMT+2). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact Israeli ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of Israel counts. Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Israeli territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Iranian ground operatives will not qualify.

question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $1,800 on March 24?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.
